title: Return to the LORD and the night visions begin
summary_text: Zechariah calls the returned community to turn back to the LORD instead of repeating the failures of their fathers. The first night vision shows horsemen patrolling the earth, and the LORD answers with zeal for Jerusalem and Judah. The chapter ends with the horns and craftsmen vision, promising that oppressive powers will not have the last word.
key_points: The people are told not to repeat their fathers' disobedience.
The horsemen report that the earth is at rest.
God announces zeal for Jerusalem and Zion.
Four horns and four craftsmen symbolize the defeat of oppressors.
evidence_phrases: I am very jealous for Jerusalem

title: Jerusalem measured and secured
summary_text: A man with a measuring line goes out to measure Jerusalem, but the city is promised a future beyond walls because the LORD himself will be its protection. The nations that plundered Zion are warned, and the call goes out for God's people to come out and be gathered. The chapter looks forward to a city filled with the LORD's presence.
key_points: Jerusalem is measured for future expansion.
The city will be protected by God's presence.
The nations that touched Zion are warned.
Many nations will join themselves to the LORD.
evidence_phrases: Jerusalem shall be inhabited as villages without walls
run, speak to this young man
I will be to her a wall of fire
I come, and I will dwell

title: Joshua cleansed and the Branch promised
summary_text: Joshua the high priest stands accused in filthy garments, but the angel of the LORD removes his iniquity and clothes him for service. The chapter then points beyond Joshua to the coming Branch who will remove sin in a single day. It is a vision of priestly cleansing and future restoration.
key_points: Joshua is defended against accusation.
His filthy garments are removed.
A clean turban and priestly robes are restored.
The Branch is promised as a future servant.
evidence_phrases: a brand plucked out of the fire
I have caused your iniquity to pass away

title: The lampstand, the olive trees, and Zerubbabel
summary_text: Zechariah sees a golden lampstand fed by two olive trees and learns that the work will not be completed by human strength alone. Zerubbabel is told that the mountain will become level and that the capstone will be set in place by God's enabling power. The chapter centers the rebuilding on the Spirit rather than on force.
key_points: The lampstand is sustained by two olive trees.
Zerubbabel is told not to despise small beginnings.
The mountain before the builder will be leveled.
The work will be finished by God's Spirit.
evidence_phrases: Not by might, nor by power
what are these two olive trees
who has despised the day of small things
he shall bring forth the headstone

title: The flying scroll and the basket of wickedness
summary_text: The prophet sees a flying scroll that goes out as a curse against thieves and perjurers, showing that hidden sin will be judged. A basket carrying wickedness is then sent away to Shinar, where it is housed far from the restored land. The chapter removes corruption from the community in symbolic and dramatic ways.
key_points: The flying scroll brings judgment on specific sins.
Wickedness is represented in a basket.
The basket is carried away from the land.
Shinar becomes the place of removal.

title: Four chariots and Joshua crowned
summary_text: Four chariots emerge from between bronze mountains, showing the LORD's judgments moving through the earth. Zechariah then crowns Joshua with silver and gold as a sign of the coming Branch who will build the temple and rule in peace. The chapter joins prophetic vision to a symbolic act that points beyond Joshua himself.
key_points: The four chariots go forth from God's presence.
A crown is placed on Joshua as a sign.
The Branch will build the temple of the LORD.
Those far off will come and help build.

title: True fasting and true justice
summary_text: When men ask whether the fasts should continue, the LORD answers that ritual without mercy and justice never satisfied him. He recalls how the fathers ignored the prophets and hardened their hearts, which brought the exile upon them. The chapter insists that obedience matters more than performance.
key_points: The people ask about their traditional fasts.
God rejects ritual divorced from obedience.
The former generation refused the prophets.
Justice, mercy, and compassion are required.

title: The LORD restores Zion and turns fasting to joy
summary_text: The LORD announces that he is jealous for Zion and will bring old men, old women, boys, and girls back into Jerusalem's streets. He promises that the fasts of the exile will become seasons of gladness and that nations will grasp the garment of a Jew to seek God. The chapter transforms grief into visible, communal restoration.
key_points: God promises to restore Zion with zeal.
Jerusalem will again be full of old and young.
Fasts will become feasts of joy.
Many nations will seek the LORD through his people.
evidence_phrases: the fast of the fifth month

title: The humble king comes to Zion
summary_text: Zechariah announces judgment on the surrounding powers and then turns to the coming king who will enter Jerusalem humble and riding on a donkey. His rule brings peace to the nations and hope to prisoners who wait in God's stronghold. The chapter presents a king whose victory is marked by humility rather than war.
key_points: The surrounding nations are judged.
The king comes to Jerusalem in humility.
War equipment is removed from his people.
Prisoners are invited to hope in deliverance.
evidence_phrases: humble and riding on a donkey
I will cut off the chariot

title: The LORD strengthens Judah and Joseph
summary_text: The chapter begins with a call to ask rain from the LORD rather than from idols or diviners. It then promises that the LORD will strengthen the house of Judah and the house of Joseph, whistle for them, and restore them from scattering. The result is a people made strong again after being broken by false shepherds.
key_points: God is the source of rain and provision.
Idols and diviners are rejected.
Judah and Joseph are gathered and strengthened.
The scattered people are made mighty again.
evidence_phrases: I will strengthen the house of Judah
they will be as though I had not cast them off
they will walk in his name

title: The flock rejects the good shepherd
summary_text: Zechariah acts out the role of a shepherd over a doomed flock, but the flock's leaders and buyers despise him. He breaks the staffs called Favor and Union, then receives thirty pieces of silver as contemptuous wages. The chapter ends with a foolish shepherd who will exploit the flock after the true shepherd is rejected.
key_points: The prophet shepherds a flock marked for slaughter.
The staffs Favor and Union are broken.
Thirty pieces of silver are thrown back in contempt.
A foolish shepherd takes over after rejection.
evidence_phrases: I broke my staff called Favor

title: Jerusalem mourns for the pierced one
summary_text: The LORD makes Jerusalem a burdensome stone to the nations and defends his city against surrounding armies. He pours out a spirit of grace and supplication on the house of David, and the people mourn as one who grieves for an only son. The chapter joins deliverance with deep repentance.
key_points: Jerusalem becomes a burdensome stone to attackers.
The LORD protects his city from siege.
A spirit of grace and supplication is poured out.
The people mourn over the one they pierced.
evidence_phrases: they will look to me whom they pierced

title: A fountain opened and the shepherd struck
summary_text: A fountain is opened for sin and uncleanness, and idolatry and false prophecy are cut out of the land. Then the LORD declares that the shepherd will be struck and the sheep scattered, but a refined remnant will remain. The chapter moves from cleansing to testing and finally to purified survival.
key_points: A fountain is opened for sin and impurity.
Idols and false prophets are removed.
The shepherd is struck and the sheep scatter.
The remnant is refined through fire.
evidence_phrases: I will cut off the names of the idols

title: The LORD reigns over all the earth
summary_text: The final chapter describes a day when the nations gather against Jerusalem, the Mount of Olives splits, and living waters flow out from the city. The LORD becomes king over all the earth, and even the common objects of worship are marked as holy. The book closes with judgment on the nations and holiness filling every corner of life.
key_points: The nations gather against Jerusalem.
The Mount of Olives is split in two.
Living waters flow from the city.
The LORD is king over all the earth.
